TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: azua se Tjauvalivali a vavayavayan sakamaya, neka nu uqaɫay.
S.PHON: azua sə cauvaɭivaɭi a vavajavajan sakamaja, nəka nu uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: azua se Tjauvalivali a vavayavayan sakamaya, neka nu uqaljay.
S.PHON: azua sə ʦauvaɣivaɣi a vavajavajan sakamaja, nəka nu uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: The people of Tjauvalivali were only females, no male.

S.FORM: saka "saɫinga tu puaɫak itjen," aya.
S.PHON: saka "saʎiŋa tu puaʎak icən," aja.
S.FORM: saka "saljinga tu pualjak itjen," aya.
S.PHON: saka "saʎiŋa tu puaʎak iʦən," aja.
S.TRANSL: They said: “We want to have children.”

S.FORM: sa sema gadu tjemuguɫudj a pavalivali.
S.PHON: sa səma gadu cəmuguʎuɉ a pavaɭivaɭi.
S.FORM: sa sema gadu tjemuguljudj a pavalivali.
S.PHON: sa səma gadu ʦəmuguʎuɉ a pavaɣivaɣi.
S.TRANSL: And they went to the mountain and bent down (?) and let the wind blow on them.

S.FORM: saka mavunglay, sa puaɫak.
S.PHON: saka mavuŋɭaj, sa puaʎak.
S.FORM: saka mavunglay, sa pualjak.
S.PHON: saka mavuŋɣaj, sa puaʎak.
S.TRANSL: They became pregnant and had children.

S.FORM: manu vavayan sakamaya, saka mapiɫay sakamaya; inika venala.
S.PHON: manu vavajan sakamaja, saka mapiʎaj sakamaja; inika vənaɭa.
S.FORM: manu vavayan sakamaya, saka mapiljay sakamaya; inika venala.
S.PHON: manu vavajan sakamaja, saka mapiʎaj sakamaja; inika vənaɣa.
S.TRANSL: But they were all female; and they were all crippled, not strong.

S.FORM: manu mumaɫ a sema gadu a tjemuguɫudj.
S.PHON: manu mumaʎ a səma gadu a cəmuguʎuɉ.
S.FORM: manu mumalj a sema gadu a tjemuguljudj.
S.PHON: manu mumaʎ a səma gadu a ʦəmuguʎuɉ.
S.TRANSL: So they went to the mountain again and bent over.

S.FORM: saka izua a qemaɫuqaɫup se Tjauzayazaya.
S.PHON: saka izua a qəmaʎuqaʎup sə cauzajazaja.
S.FORM: saka izua a qemaljuqaljup se Tjauzayazaya.
S.PHON: saka izua a qəmaʎuqaʎup sə ʦauzajazaja.
S.TRANSL: And there was a man of Tjauzayazaya out hunting.

S.FORM: saka ɫevavaw tua kasiw a ɫemizaw.
S.PHON: saka ʎəvavaw tua kasiw a ʎəmizaw.
S.FORM: saka ljevavaw tua kasiw a ljemizaw.
S.PHON: saka ʎəvavaw tua kasiw a ʎəmizaw.
S.TRANSL: He climbed a tree to have a look,

S.FORM: manu izua a vavayavayan a natjemuguɫudj.
S.PHON: manu izua a vavajavajan a nacəmuguʎuɉ.
S.FORM: manu izua a vavayavayan a natjemuguljudj.
S.PHON: manu izua a vavajavajan a naʦəmuguʎuɉ.
S.TRANSL: and there were the women bending over.

S.FORM: saka ɫemengɫeng azua uqaɫay.
S.PHON: saka ʎəməŋʎəŋ azua uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: saka ljemengljeng azua uqaljay.
S.PHON: saka ʎəməŋʎəŋ azua uqaʎaj.

S.FORM: "aku izua vavayan a namaitazua?" aya.
S.PHON: "aku izua vavajan a namaitazua?" aja.
S.FORM: "aku izua vavayan a namaitazua?" aya.
S.PHON: "aku izua vavajan a namaitazua?" aja.
S.TRANSL: and said: “Why are the women doing that?”

S.FORM: saka paɫiɫi.
S.PHON: saka paʎiʎi.
S.FORM: saka paljilji.
S.PHON: saka paʎiʎi.

S.FORM: manu tjenglay tua uqaɫay azua vavayavayan; saka djemadjas saka puzekatj.
S.PHON: manu cəŋɭaj tua uqaʎaj azua vavajavajan; saka ɉəmaɉas saka puzəkac.
S.FORM: manu tjenglay tua uqaljay azua vavayavayan; saka djemadjas saka puzekatj.
S.PHON: manu ʦəŋɣaj tua uqaʎaj azua vavajavajan; saka ɉəmaɉas saka puzəkaʦ.
S.TRANSL: The women liked the man, and got hold of him and had intercourse (?).

S.FORM: sa puaɫak tua uqaɫay.
S.PHON: sa puaʎak tua uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: sa pualjak tua uqaljay.
S.PHON: sa puaʎak tua uqaʎaj.

S.FORM: saka mirazek a kitjuqinaɫan, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: saka mirazək a kicuqinaʎan, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: saka mirazek a kitjuqinaljan, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: saka miRazək a kiʦuqinaʎan, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: And so they flourished as a separate village, according to the story.
